title: AFI ScoredSignalEvidence v3 (EV3-CONTRACT — canonical scored-signal evidence contract with provider invocation provenance)
description: The canonical scored-signal evidence record contract, v3 — the SOLE current canonical scored-signal evidence contract (EV3-GOV D-EV3-1). It is the v2 record shape carried forward UNCHANGED (the same reused afi.scored-signal.v1 / afi.provenance-record.v1 / CanonicalHash / afi.composition-ref.v1 shapes, the same twelve required and two optional v2 properties, the same lifecycle/finality binder) PLUS EXACTLY THREE new REQUIRED properties: providerInvocations (the five-lane provider invocation proof collection, D-EV3-2), recordHash (the full-record integrity commitment, D-EV3-4), and replayHash (the deterministic semantic/replay commitment, D-EV3-4). v3 is the ACTIVE WRITE CONTRACT and the ONLY current evidence contract: no dual mode, no feature-flagged fallback, no compatibility alias; every active-tree surface of the prior versions is deleted forward-only under D-EV3-8 (git history and the accepted governance ledger are the archive). It remains a THIN, self-identifying evidence record keyed by the governed signalId (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-1) that CARRIES BY REUSE the canonical afi.scored-signal.v1 projection (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-5) and its afi.provenance-record.v1 (present per LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-6; replay/verify sufficiency per MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-9), the canonical strategy identity triple (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-3), explicit schema/object versions (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-6), the minimum lifecycle/finality fields (LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-1/D-LIFE-4/D-LIFE-6, MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-5), and the required composition reference (FCP-GOV). It is NOT the heavy ReactorScoredSignalDocument and NOT the full VaultedSignalRecord lifecycle brain: runtime/storage baggage (rawUss, lenses, _priceFeedMetadata, rawPayload, volatile processing timestamps) and validator/minted/replayed/training/proprietary lifecycle-brain fields are structurally rejected (additionalProperties:false, and the reused sub-schemas are themselves additionalProperties:false).
$comment: BOUNDARY: schema/contract only — NO storage engine, NO collection names, NO indexes, NO deployment topology, NO migration mechanics, NO API/endpoint. Store-layer requirements that JSON Schema cannot enforce (signalId uniqueness, append-once, immutable-after-FINALIZED, single-writer boundary, operational-vs-canonical separation, recomputation-verified admission per D-EV3-7) are recorded as governed contract constraints in x-afiConstraints and enforced by the accompanying contract-constraint tests and the sole writer, NOT by index/DDL here. Cross-object identifier continuity (top-level signalId == scoredSignal.signalId == provenanceRecord.signalId; strategy-triple agreement) and the D-EV3-5(3) cross-check laws also cannot be enforced by JSON Schema draft-07 and are governed contract constraints (x-afiConstraints) verified by the drift-guard tests and the fail-closed District Two builder. SUPERSESSION (EV3-GOV D-EV3-1/D-EV3-8): v3 is the sole current contract; the prior v1/v2 versions ceased to be active contracts and their in-tree surfaces are DELETED forward-only — no dual-write, no back-write, no archive directory, no deprecation marker; git history and the accepted governance ledger are the archive.
x-afiPartOf: AFI Evidence V3 and Provider Invocation Provenance v0.1 (EV3-CONTRACT); supersedes the FACTORY-CONTRACT v2 evidence record as the active write contract
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-1 — authorizes afi.scored-signal-evidence.v3 (the v2 record shape carried forward unchanged plus exactly three additions: providerInvocations, recordHash, replayHash) as the SOLE current canonical evidence contract; no dual V2/V3 mode, no fallback, no alias

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-2 — the per-lane provider invocation proof (afi.provider-invocation-proof.v1): exactly five, closed, unique by category, deterministically ordered ascending case-sensitive (aiMl, news, pattern, sentiment, technical), credential-safe, carried-not-consumed

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-3 — the aiMl nested invocation proof (afi.aiml-invocation-proof.v1), required exactly on the aiMl proof, structurally forbidden elsewhere

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-4 — hash law: recordHash (domain afi.d2.evidence-record) and replayHash (domain afi.d2.evidence-replay) computed under the composition canonicalization law (canonical-json-hashing.v1) with the closed preimage projections stated in x-afiConstraints; PR-O1 resolved (per-lane commitments in the proofs' categoryResultHash values, the single bundle enrichmentHash retained at the composition layer); explicit canonical/replay projection separation

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-5 — a scored evaluation requires ALL FIVE lanes to succeed (froggy-trend-pullback v1.3.0 fail-fast manifest); invocation facts are captured in the one live graph pass; exactly one District Two Evidence V3 builder, fail-closed on every mismatch

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-6 — credential-safety law: enumerated negative proofs; opaque CredentialRef facts or explicit keyless posture only; structural exclusion plus synthetic-marker test obligation

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-7 — the canonical store admits ONLY v3 on the existing store surface; recordHash/replayHash verified by recomputation before insert; no migration framework (MONGO-IMPL Slot 5 unexercised)

afi-governance/decisions/evidence-v3-provider-provenance-v0.1 (EV3-GOV) D-EV3-8 — forward-only replacement: every active-tree V1/V2 evidence surface deleted after proof; no compatibility shims, aliases, dual-run modes, or tombstones

afi-governance/decisions/factory-configurable-pipelines-v1 (FCP-GOV) D-FCP-3 — the contract-family change-control rule this version satisfies ('any shape change requires a new governance decision and a new schema version'); the required composition reference is carried forward unchanged
afi-governance/decisions/persistence-impl-v0.1.md (MONGO-IMPL) Slot 1 — authorized the original evidence contract whose core this version carries forward; boundary is schema/contract only
afi-governance/decisions/persistence-v0.1.md (MONGO-GOV) D-MONGO-1 — one canonical scored-signal evidence store & its self-identifying persisted shape
afi-governance/decisions/persistence-v0.1.md (MONGO-GOV) D-MONGO-2 — afi-config owns the canonical persisted-record schema contract
afi-governance/decisions/persistence-v0.1.md (MONGO-GOV) D-MONGO-5 — write-once/append-only per signalId; immutable-after-FINALIZED; versioning by supersession
afi-governance/decisions/persistence-v0.1.md (MONGO-GOV) D-MONGO-6 — signalId uniqueness/idempotency at the store layer
afi-governance/decisions/persistence-v0.1.md (MONGO-GOV) D-MONGO-9 — replay/verify sufficiency data guarantees on the record; the invocation proofs and record hashes strengthen this guarantee
afi-governance/decisions/object-identity-v0.1.md (OBJ-GOV) D-OBJ-1 — signalId canonical end-to-end join key
afi-governance/decisions/object-identity-v0.1.md (OBJ-GOV) D-OBJ-3 — canonical strategy triple analystId + strategyId + strategyVersion
afi-governance/decisions/object-identity-v0.1.md (OBJ-GOV) D-OBJ-5 — canonical Scored Signal representation = thin afi.scored-signal.v1 projection
afi-governance/decisions/object-identity-v0.1.md (OBJ-GOV) D-OBJ-6 — two-axis versioning (schema-id const + distinct canonicalizationVersion)
afi-governance/decisions/lifecycle-v0.1.md (LIFE-GOV) D-LIFE-1 — one canonical lifecycle state machine
afi-governance/decisions/lifecycle-v0.1.md (LIFE-GOV) D-LIFE-4 — single finality writer; FINALIZED is a lifecycle-finality event (not settlement finality)
afi-governance/decisions/lifecycle-v0.1.md (LIFE-GOV) D-LIFE-5 — identifier continuity: every lifecycle record keyed on signalId + carries the strategy triple + references afi.scored-signal.v1

afi-governance/decisions/lifecycle-v0.1.md (LIFE-GOV) D-LIFE-6 — pre-persistence handoff: VALIDATED + SCORED (afi.scored-signal.v1 projection AND its ProvenanceRecord) + identifier continuity before entry into this store

afi-governance/decisions/uwr-profile-pin-v0.1.md (PR-UWR-STAMP) UP-2/UP-5 — the scoring-profile stamp SHAPE (profileId + status + decisionRef) this record reuses verbatim. Stamping is traceability metadata only: it does NOT wire the qualification gate (UP-9), create reward eligibility, or promote production scoring law (§6). This contract fixes no analyst, strategy, or profile value: UP-10 profile RECOGNITION scopes which profile a given implementation may stamp, and is an implementation/runtime property — it is NOT an admission rule of this analyst-neutral contract.

afi-governance/decisions/uwr-runtime-consumption-v0.1.md (PR-UWR-STAMP-SEMANTICS) RC-4/RC-5/RC-6 — the stamp's source discriminator values (builtin-value-identity | registry-consumed), reused EXACTLY; fail-closed runtime resolution (a failed/invalid profile resolution refuses to score, so no record exists to stamp); and RC-6's reserved meaning for an ABSENT source (identifies the pre-program era). No new stamp semantics are introduced here.

x-afiConstraints.storeUniqueness: STORE-LAYER (not JSON-Schema-enforceable): the canonical evidence store MUST enforce signalId uniqueness on the single CURRENT canonical record per signalId (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-6 — unique constraint / insert-if-absent). Re-ingesting the same signalId MUST NOT create a duplicate canonical record. The store type MUST support a unique signalId constraint (i.e. a standard collection, NOT time-series). This contract fixes signalId as the idempotency key; the index/DDL/collection is MONGO-STORE, not this schema.
x-afiConstraints.appendOnceImmutableAfterFinalized: STORE-LAYER (not JSON-Schema-enforceable): the record is write-once/append-only per signalId (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-5). One CURRENT record per signalId; a governed correction SUPERSEDES the current record with a new schema-versioned record (recordVersion increments; superseded versions retained immutably as history). Once lifecycleState reaches FINALIZED, the record is immutable (finalized === true marks this). Immutability here is STORAGE custody, NOT settlement finality (CHAIN-GOV).
x-afiConstraints.singleWriterBoundary: STORE-LAYER (not JSON-Schema-enforceable): only the afi-infra canonical persistence interface may write this record (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-3). Reactor and Gateway are SUBMITTERS, not writers. A record is admissible only when it carries the afi.scored-signal.v1 projection + provenance linkage + signalId continuity, satisfies the LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-6 handoff, and passes the D-EV3-7 recomputation-verified admission (recordHash and replayHash recomputed and matched before insert; an invalid or mis-hashed record is rejected, never persisted). This contract does not define that interface (ATLAS-GOV / MONGO-STORE), only the shape it transports.
x-afiConstraints.operationalVsCanonical: This record is CANONICAL evidence only (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-4). Raw/unscored ingestion, intermediate/DAG state, retry/dead-letter, replay inputs, dedupe caches, and telemetry are OPERATIONAL stores and MUST NOT be represented by this contract. Wall-clock timings, attempt counts, retry/rate-limit facts, and transport noise are NON-CANONICAL operational diagnostics and MUST NOT appear anywhere in this record or in any hashed preimage (EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(7)). The composition ref and the invocation proofs carry HASH PINS and identity facts — never the artifacts, traces, or intermediate payloads themselves.
x-afiConstraints.identifierContinuity: CROSS-OBJECT (not JSON-Schema-draft-07-enforceable): the top-level signalId MUST equal scoredSignal.signalId AND provenanceRecord.signalId (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-1, LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-5). The top-level strategy triple (analystId, strategyId, strategyVersion) — all three REQUIRED-present on this evidence record — MUST equal the scoredSignal triple (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-3). The top-level canonicalizationVersion MUST equal provenanceRecord.canonicalizationVersion (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-6). Enforced by the drift-guard contract tests.
x-afiConstraints.uwrProfileStamp: SCORING-PROFILE STAMP, RUNTIME-LAYER (not JSON-Schema-enforceable): the uwrProfile stamp reuses the already-governed PR-UWR-STAMP shape and the PR-UWR-STAMP-SEMANTICS RC-6 source discriminator VERBATIM — this contract adds no stamp semantics, redesigns no UWR, changes no profile value, and does not reopen UP-8/UP-9. The runtime submitter MUST: (a) stamp the profile that ACTUALLY produced the score — never a profile it did not run; (b) build `source` from the provenance the composition path ACTUALLY resolved, propagated explicitly — never re-derived at the stamp site, never read from the environment (RC-6); (c) fail closed — if the profile it was configured to run cannot be resolved/validated, it must refuse to score, so no evidence record and no stamp exist for a failed resolution (RC-4 realizes this for the registry-backed profile). This contract is ANALYST-/STRATEGY-/PROFILE-NEUTRAL and binds no record to any one analyst identity or profile: which profiles a given implementation can emit, and which identities it recognizes, are properties of that implementation's pipeline/runtime — not admission rules of this contract. Enforced by the submitter's runtime guardrails, not by this schema.
x-afiConstraints.replaySufficiency: The record is structured for deterministic off-line replay/verify (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-9): it carries the afi.scored-signal.v1 projection AND the afi.provenance-record.v1, which supplies AT MINIMUM the input + output CanonicalHash digests (required by that shape), plus the enrichment digest and replay pins where present (optional on the reused shape), AND the required composition ref pinning WHAT composed the score. v3 EXTENDS the guarantee with the five provider invocation proofs (WHO/WHAT was invoked per lane, cryptographically bound to the result that entered the join) and the record-level recordHash/replayHash commitments: two evaluations of the same canonical inputs through the same composition MUST produce identical replay projections and identical replayHash values (EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(7)). This is a data guarantee on the record — NOT a read/replay endpoint (ATLAS-GOV reserved).
x-afiConstraints.compositionBinding: COMPOSITION PROVENANCE (carried forward unchanged): composition is REQUIRED on every record — a record whose composition provenance cannot be fully produced MUST NOT be written (fail closed; afi.composition-ref.v1 is itself all-or-nothing). CROSS-OBJECT agreements (tooling/submitter-enforced, not JSON-Schema-enforceable): composition.analystConfigHash MUST equal the resolved registration's analystConfigHash; composition.enrichmentHash MUST equal provenanceRecord.enrichmentHash when that optional field is present; the scorer plugin identified by composition MUST be the one that actually produced scoredSignal. SUPERSESSION (EV3-GOV D-EV3-1/D-EV3-8): v3 is the ACTIVE WRITE CONTRACT and the ONLY current evidence contract — the prior versions ceased to be active contracts, their active-tree surfaces are deleted forward-only, and there is no dual-write, no back-write, and no compatibility alias.
x-afiConstraints.providerInvocationProofs: PROVIDER INVOCATION PROVENANCE (v3 addition, EV3-GOV D-EV3-2): providerInvocations MUST contain EXACTLY FIVE afi.provider-invocation-proof.v1 objects — one per governed category, unique by category, deterministically ordered ascending by case-sensitive lexicographic category name (aiMl, news, pattern, sentiment, technical) — bound POSITIONALLY by this schema (a draft-07 five-tuple with per-position category consts, additionalItems:false), which structurally enforces count, order, uniqueness, and the aiMl position. Proof facts are CARRIED, NEVER CONSUMED: no category node, join, analyst, scorer, UWR, or decay path may read them. Facts are captured inside the one live graph pass (D-EV3-5(2)); evidence never re-calls a provider.
x-afiConstraints.recordHashLaw: RECORD INTEGRITY COMMITMENT (v3 addition, EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(6)): recordHash carries the CanonicalHash v1 (domain afi.d2.evidence-record, tag CARRIED not hashed) of the FULL v3 record MINUS {recordHash, replayHash}, computed under the composition canonicalization law canonical-json-hashing.v1 (sha256 over UTF-8 of the canonically serialized JSON; top-level exclusion of exactly those two fields). The store verifies it by recomputation before insert (D-EV3-7); a mismatch is rejected, never persisted. This also gives the pre-existing supersedesRecordHash link its defined computation for v3 records.
x-afiConstraints.replayHashLaw: DETERMINISTIC SEMANTIC/REPLAY COMMITMENT (v3 addition, EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(6)/(7)): replayHash carries the CanonicalHash v1 (domain afi.d2.evidence-replay, tag CARRIED not hashed) of the REPLAY PROJECTION — the record MINUS {recordHash, replayHash, lifecycleState, finalized, recordVersion, supersedesRecordHash} — computed under canonical-json-hashing.v1. Two evaluations of the same canonical inputs through the same composition MUST produce identical replay projections and identical replayHash values; lifecycle progression and supersession custody NEVER move replayHash. No volatile or operational timestamp or duration may enter either preimage; domain-declared normalized evidence timestamps remain admissible exactly as the District 2 hash doctrine provides.
x-afiConstraints.crossCheckLaws: FAIL-CLOSED CROSS-CHECKS (builder-enforced, EV3-GOV D-EV3-5(3), not JSON-Schema-enforceable): the five categoryResultHash values MUST equal the recomputed composition-law hashes of the category results the analyst path ACTUALLY consumed at the join; every provider/instance/adapter/credential identity fact MUST equal the boot-verified governed registry resolution of the invocation that occurred; the aiMl proof's Tiny Brains projection MUST pass its profile/resolver/expert cross-checks (including the KAT-proven outputHash recomputation at the service boundary, D-EV3-4(5)). A missing, duplicate, unknown, or mis-ordered proof, any identity or hash mismatch, or any schema-validation failure yields NO scored V3 record — never a fabricated proof, never a downgraded record, never a prior-version write.

schema.description: Schema-id version of the evidence record (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-6 axis (a)). v3 = the v2 shape carried forward unchanged + required providerInvocations, recordHash, replayHash (EV3-GOV D-EV3-1). Versions the RECORD shape, distinct from canonicalizationVersion.

signalId.description: The canonical join key (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-1): stable, opaque, assigned once at the USS ingest boundary, threaded verbatim, immutable. This is the store idempotency key (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-6). MUST equal scoredSignal.signalId and provenanceRecord.signalId (identifier continuity, LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-5).

analystId.description: Canonical strategy triple member (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-3): the analyst, kept orthogonal to strategyId, never folded into it. MUST equal scoredSignal.analystId.

strategyId.description: Canonical strategy triple member (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-3): the strategy identifier (bare snake_case slug with embedded major-version token; must not embed the analyst name). MUST equal scoredSignal.strategyId. This contract fixes NO strategy value/format beyond OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-3; production scoring law is untouched.

strategyVersion.description: REQUIRED canonical strategy triple member (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-3): finer semver-without-v-prefix version, separate from strategyId. The canonical evidence record MUST carry the COMPLETE canonical strategy identity triple (analystId + strategyId + strategyVersion), so this field is required on the evidence contract even though D-OBJ-3 leaves it optional at the general object-identity level and the reused afi.scored-signal.v1 projection keeps it optional. MUST equal scoredSignal.strategyVersion (identifier continuity). Format is unchanged from D-OBJ-3 (non-binding, illustrative semver e.g. 1.0.0); this is a presence requirement on the evidence record, not a redefinition of the governed format or of the projection's own optionality.

canonicalizationVersion.description: Two-axis versioning axis (b) (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-6): the canonical-serialization rules version, held DISTINCT from the schema-id. MUST agree with provenanceRecord.canonicalizationVersion (enforced as a governed identifier-continuity constraint; see x-afiConstraints.identifierContinuity); the canonicalizationVersion inside each contained CanonicalHash is expected to match by the same doctrine. This field pins the version only; hash/serialization rules are District-2 / CHAIN-GOV doctrine, not set here.

lifecycleState.description: The canonical LIFE-GOV lifecycle state (D-LIFE-1) of this signal, as a lifecycle attribute of the evidence record (LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-5). Restricted to the PERSISTABLE states: a record only exists post-scoring (LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-6 handoff requires VALIDATED + SCORED), so pre-scoring states (INGESTED, VALIDATED, SCHEMA_REJECTED) are NOT admissible here. Shipped tier-4 vocabularies (afi-mint pending/minted, afi-infra RAW/ENRICHED/ANALYZED/SCORED-caps/MINTED/REPLAYED, decay_pass/challenge_open/voting_complete) are NOT this canonical set. Mint/settlement states are CHAIN-GOV, beyond EPOCH_ELIGIBLE, and not representable here. EXCLUDED from the replayHash preimage (x-afiConstraints.replayHashLaw): lifecycle progression never moves the replay commitment.

finalized.description: The immutable-after-FINALIZED marker (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-5). MUST be true iff lifecycleState is a finalization-phase-or-later state (FINALIZED, FINAL_REJECTED, EPOCH_ELIGIBLE) — bound structurally by if/then/else below. Marks storage-custody immutability (append-once, supersede-only), NOT on-chain settlement finality (CHAIN-GOV). FINALIZED/FINAL_REJECTED are written only by the LIFE-GOV finality writer (D-LIFE-4); this contract records the marker, it does not name or wire that writer. EXCLUDED from the replayHash preimage (x-afiConstraints.replayHashLaw).

scoredSignal.description: The canonical thin afi.scored-signal.v1 projection (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-5), carried BY REUSE of the governed schema (not redefined). Its additionalProperties:false structurally forbids substituting the heavy ReactorScoredSignalDocument (rawUss/lenses/_priceFeedMetadata/rawPayload) for the thin projection.

provenanceRecord.description: The afi.provenance-record.v1 required PRESENT by LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-6, carried BY REUSE of the governed schema. Supplies the input/enrichment/output CanonicalHash digests + replay pins for MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-9 replay/verify sufficiency. Its full canonical shape remains a deferred afi-config schema (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-6 fixes only its versioning/continuity-linkage role); this contract reuses the existing District-2 draft shape and does not re-decide it (its PR-O1 open item is RESOLVED by EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(4) as a metadata annotation — the shape is unchanged).

uwrProfile.description: REQUIRED scoring-profile stamp: identifies the UWR/scoring profile that ACTUALLY produced this score, and — via `source` — its exact provenance, using the already-governed discriminator semantics (PR-UWR-STAMP, uwr-profile-pin-v0.1.md §7; source discriminator per PR-UWR-STAMP-SEMANTICS RC-6). The stamp shape and the `source` values are reused VERBATIM; no new stamp semantics are defined here. Traceability metadata ONLY: it does NOT indicate qualification, reward eligibility, or mint wiring — each remains separately authorized (UP-9/§6). REQUIRED on every newly created canonical evidence record: the canonical store is FRESH (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-1, Option A) and holds no pre-program records, so it carries no compatibility obligation to accept a newly written UNSTAMPED record. ANALYST-/STRATEGY-/PROFILE-NEUTRAL: this contract fixes NO analyst, strategy, or profile value. Any AFI-conforming profile may be stamped, provided the profile is identified and its source is one of the governed discriminator values. Which profiles a given implementation can actually emit is an implementation/runtime property — NOT a restriction of this protocol contract, and no bespoke governance decision is required merely for an analyst to configure and run a conforming strategy. Governance remains required for protocol-wide canonical defaults, registry admission rules, reward eligibility, production scoring law, and economic effects — none of which this stamp confers.

profileId.description: Identifies the UWR/scoring profile that ACTUALLY produced this score (UP-2 stamp shape), e.g. "uwr-weighted-lifts-v0.1" — illustrative only. ANALYST-NEUTRAL: this contract fixes NO profile value and imposes no format beyond non-empty; any AFI-conforming profile id is admissible. Which profile ids a given implementation can emit is an implementation/runtime property, not a restriction of this contract.

status.description: The declared governance/lifecycle status of the stamped profile (e.g. "testnet-provisional"), recorded so persisted records self-describe. ANALYST-NEUTRAL: this contract requires the field to be present and well-formed but fixes NO status vocabulary — it does not pin any one profile's current status as the only admissible value. Which statuses are meaningful is a property of the stamped profile, not of this record contract.

decisionRef.description: The defining reference for the stamped profile — the document that specifies/pins it (e.g. "afi-governance/decisions/uwr-profile-pin-v0.1.md", illustrative only). Part of the reused stamp shape, kept for provenance traceability. ANALYST-NEUTRAL: this contract fixes no value and does NOT require a bespoke AFI governance decision for a profile to be stampable — a conforming profile supplies whatever reference defines it. Governance remains required for protocol-wide canonical defaults, registry admission rules, reward eligibility, production scoring law, and economic effects — none of which this field confers.

source.description: REQUIRED RC-6 source discriminator (PR-UWR-STAMP-SEMANTICS), reusing the governed values EXACTLY. "builtin-value-identity": scoring ran afi-core's builtin defaultUwrConfig — value-identical to the registered profile by construction (UP-2/UP-5) — and the registry was NOT read. "registry-consumed": scoring ran the profile actually READ from the afi-config registry and validated under the RC-5 identity predicate. Runtime resolution is fail-closed (RC-4): a failed/invalid registry read refuses to score, so no evidence record — and no stamp — can exist for a failed resolution. REQUIRED here because RC-6 reserves an ABSENT source to identify the PRE-PROGRAM era: this fresh canonical store contains no pre-program records, so a newly written stamp without a source would masquerade as pre-program and is rejected. (The pre-program allowance remains governed for reading historical records in the demoted legacy stores — it is not relevant to what this store admits.)

composition.description: REQUIRED composition provenance (carried forward unchanged from the v2 shape): the complete, hash-pinned identity of the composition that produced this score — pipeline manifest, analyst config, scorer plugin, plugin set, execution summary, enrichment bundle — carried BY REUSE of afi.composition-ref.v1 (all fields required there; all-or-nothing). A record whose composition provenance cannot be fully produced MUST NOT be written (x-afiConstraints.compositionBinding).

providerInvocations.description: REQUIRED five-lane provider invocation proof collection (v3 addition, EV3-GOV D-EV3-2): EXACTLY FIVE afi.provider-invocation-proof.v1 objects, one per governed category, unique by category, deterministically ordered ascending by case-sensitive lexicographic category name — position 0 aiMl, 1 news, 2 pattern, 3 sentiment, 4 technical. The positional five-tuple below structurally enforces count, order, uniqueness, and the aiMl position (and therefore the nested afi.aiml-invocation-proof.v1 at position 0). Proof facts are carried, never consumed (x-afiConstraints.providerInvocationProofs).

recordHash.description: REQUIRED full-record integrity commitment (v3 addition, EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(6)): CanonicalHash v1, domain afi.d2.evidence-record, over the full record MINUS {recordHash, replayHash} under the composition canonicalization law (x-afiConstraints.recordHashLaw). Verified by recomputation before insert (D-EV3-7).

replayHash.description: REQUIRED deterministic semantic/replay commitment (v3 addition, EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(6)/(7)): CanonicalHash v1, domain afi.d2.evidence-replay, over the replay projection — the record MINUS {recordHash, replayHash, lifecycleState, finalized, recordVersion, supersedesRecordHash} — under the composition canonicalization law (x-afiConstraints.replayHashLaw). Identical canonical inputs through the same composition yield identical values.

recordVersion.description: OPTIONAL supersession counter for versioning-by-supersession (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-5). The first canonical record for a signalId is version 1 (omission means 1); a governed correction supersedes with an incremented recordVersion and a new schema-versioned record. Never an in-place edit; never relaxes the one-current-record-per-signalId invariant. EXCLUDED from the replayHash preimage (x-afiConstraints.replayHashLaw).

supersedesRecordHash.description: OPTIONAL CanonicalHash link to the immediately-superseded canonical evidence record (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-5 supersession chain). Present only when recordVersion > 1; the superseded record is retained immutably as history. Its defined computation for v3 records is the superseded record's recordHash (EV3-GOV D-EV3-4(6)). EXCLUDED from the replayHash preimage (x-afiConstraints.replayHashLaw).
